focus: Angle 1: Layer-1b half-landed vocabulary and removable code-audit penalty
noveltyJustification: First pass established the concrete metadata and scorer coupling for code-audit phrasing.
keyQuestions: Which deep-loop vocabulary still attracts single-pass code-audit prompts?
Which hardcoded scorer penalties compensate for that metadata?
answeredQuestions: deep-loop-workflows still projects iterative code audit, severity weighted findings, and code audit in derived trigger phrases.
fusion.ts still applies codeAuditDeepReviewPenalty when prompt text contains code audit.

ruledOut.approach: Remove scorer penalty before metadata cleanup
ruledOut.reason: The metadata still emits the contested phrase, so removing the penalty first would regress code-audit routing.

focus: Angle 2: Vocabulary authority split between explicit code constants and graph metadata
noveltyJustification: Mapped the double-counting surfaces and proposed an ownership contract instead of another local penalty.
keyQuestions: What belongs in TOKEN_BOOSTS/PHRASE_BOOSTS vs graph metadata?
Where can double-counting happen before clamps?
answeredQuestions: Explicit lane hardcodes broad token and phrase boosts, then also consumes every skill intent signal and keyword.
Derived lane scores graph-derived triggers and keywords separately, so hub metadata can add another lane of evidence for the same phrase.

ruledOut.approach: Move all vocabulary into graph metadata
ruledOut.reason: Syntax anchors like slash commands and colon commands need code-level exactness and command bridge handling.

noveltyJustification: Found the current guard is single-hub and designed a report that spans parent hubs and projection surfaces.
keyQuestions: Does parent-hub-vocab-sync compare across hubs?
What collision classes should block?
answeredQuestions: The guard accepts one skillRoot and builds one hub-local projection.
Alias collisions are currently only mode collisions within one hub.

ruledOut.reason: The existing guard normalizes case, punctuation, hyphen, and whitespace; cross-hub reporting should reuse the same normalization.

focus: Angle 4: Advisor projection surface validation beyond trigger_phrases
noveltyJustification: Connected the guard gap to projection.ts and derived.ts, showing key_topics and source fields are live scoring inputs.
keyQuestions: Which graph metadata fields become advisor projection fields?
Which fields does the guard ignore today?
answeredQuestions: projection.ts builds derivedTriggers from derived.trigger_phrases and derivedKeywords from key_topics, entities, key_files, and source_docs.
scoreDerivedLane scores derivedTriggers plus derivedKeywords, so non-trigger metadata can affect ranking.

ruledOut.approach: Require every key_file and source_doc to be typed as a user alias
ruledOut.reason: Executor alias derivation intentionally excludes derived keywords because file paths and doc names over-match general prompts; the same distinction should exist for hub guard coverage.

focus: Angle 5: Ambiguous cross-hub prompt set and measured margins
noveltyJustification: Converted the top-priority compatibility questions into a labeled ambiguity fixture proposal tied to the existing ratchet slice.
keyQuestions: Which ambiguity prompts need labels?
How should the results feed 007's frozen ambiguity slice?
answeredQuestions: 007 freezes an ambiguity slice with 15/25 top-1, so new parent-hub prompts should be measured as an added named slice instead of replacing the existing one.
The top collision bands are code-audit vs deep-review, design-audit vs code-audit, runtime vs workflow, and one-shot context vs loop.

ruledOut.approach: Tune lane weights before adding labels
ruledOut.reason: Without a labeled ambiguity slice, lane-weight changes cannot distinguish true fixes from hidden regressions.

focus: Angle 6: Atomic reindex, re-baseline, ratchet-recapture runbook
noveltyJustification: Synthesized a no-partial-state runbook that respects SQLite/native fragility and the ratchet's fixture pins.
keyQuestions: What operation order avoids stale advisor projections?
How should ratchets be recaptured after metadata movement?
answeredQuestions: The ratchet scores under a frozen filesystem projection with an empty MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_DB_DIR and disabled semantic lane.
008 avoided live daemon vector DB fragility by using a fixture projection for semantic measurement.

ruledOut.approach: Directly mutate the live SQLite projection for experiments
ruledOut.reason: Existing benchmark and semantic packets avoid live DB mutation by using read-only copies or fixture projections.

focus: Angle 7: Live conflicts_with edge authoring criteria
noveltyJustification: Confirmed the scorer already consumes negative edges and proposed a measured corpus-authoring gate rather than broad seeding.
keyQuestions: Is the conflicts_with seam live in scorer code?
Which conflict edges are safe to author?
answeredQuestions: graph-causal assigns conflicts_with a negative multiplier and fusion uses a conflict rerank seam under RRF.
003 measured one corrected top-1 only when conflict mass was overlaid, not live-authored.

ruledOut.approach: Author conflicts for every overlap between hubs
ruledOut.reason: Sibling or prerequisite overlaps are not conflicts; only mutually exclusive operator intents should demote.

focus: Angle 8: Command bridge hardcoding and metadata-derived replacement
noveltyJustification: Identified a concrete precedent for metadata-derived aliases and a bounded replacement for the six hardcoded COMMAND_BRIDGES.
keyQuestions: Where are command bridges hardcoded?
What in-repo precedent can derive aliases safely?
answeredQuestions: projection.ts declares six command bridge SkillProjection objects inline.
buildExecutorAliasTable derives active aliases from projected skill metadata and excludes overbroad derived keywords.

ruledOut.approach: Generate bridges from arbitrary command markdown body text
ruledOut.reason: Unbounded prose would over-match; only frontmatter, declared aliases, and command router metadata should feed bridges.

focus: Angle 9: Crosswalk for query-class, hub-router, and eval buckets
noveltyJustification: Mapped the three taxonomy layers and proposed validation instead of forcing one to derive all others immediately.
keyQuestions: What are the three active taxonomies?
How should they stay consistent?
answeredQuestions: classifyAdvisorQuery has implementation, review, documentation, memory, tooling, unknown classes.
007 uses named buckets such as review, memory_save, and delegation for eval slices.

ruledOut.approach: Make eval buckets the only taxonomy
ruledOut.reason: Eval buckets are measurement slices; they cannot replace live query classes or hub-router mode ownership without losing runtime intent semantics.

focus: Angle 10: semantic_shadow description-embedding hygiene for mcp-* near-neighbor attractors
noveltyJustification: The final pass added a cheaper hygiene path for the known mcp-chrome-devtools abstain false-fires without proposing lane-weight changes.
keyQuestions: Why do semantic_shadow abstain false-fires matter?
What is cheaper than weight tuning?
answeredQuestions: 008 measured three gold-none abstain false-fires to mcp-chrome-devtools and retained the 0.05 freeze.
semantic vectors are built from skill name, description, domains, intentSignals, and derivedTriggers, so description/trigger hygiene can reduce attractors without changing weights.

ruledOut.approach: Raise or drop semantic_shadow weight as the first fix
ruledOut.reason: 008 measured the lane as marginal net-negative and froze weight 0.05; hygiene is the lower-blast path.
